_meta.note: Prices reflect national average ranges. Regional variation ±30%. Update every 12 months.

spark_plug_cyl1.dealer_note: Dealer charges premium labor rates; identical parts

parts.name: Spark Plug x1 (single)
parts.oem_note: Match heat range to OEM spec

uncertainty: Labor time varies significantly based on engine layout
V6 and V8 rear bank plugs can add 1+ hours
Coil boots may need replacement if cracked
estimate_wrong_if: If misfire persists after plugs, coil or injector is next
GDI engines may need intake valve cleaning which is not included here

ignition_coil_cyl1.dealer_note: Dealers often replace all coils at once — ask to replace only failed unit first
uncertainty: Price varies widely by make/model
Some engines require intake manifold removal for rear coils
estimate_wrong_if: If all coils are original at 100k+ miles, replacing one at a time may cost more than replacing all — get shop estimate for full set

vacuum_leak.dealer_note: Dealer labor rates make vacuum leak diagnosis expensive if location is not immediately obvious
vacuum_leak.diy_time: 30 min – several hours (finding the leak is the hard part)

parts.name: Intake manifold gasket (if needed)
parts.oem_note: Only if leak is at manifold seating surface

uncertainty: Location of leak dramatically affects repair cost
Smoke machine rental speeds up diagnosis significantly
Some leaks are at intake manifold gasket — repair cost jumps to $200-400
estimate_wrong_if: If leak is at intake manifold gasket, cost is substantially higher
Some vacuum leaks are only present cold or under load — intermittent leaks are harder to find

exhaust_manifold_leak.dealer_note: Exhaust manifold work on dealer's lift rates is expensive

parts.name: Exhaust manifold studs (often seized)
parts.oem_note: Budget for broken studs if vehicle is >8 years old

uncertainty: Seized/broken studs significantly increase labor time and cost
Older vehicles in rust belt have much higher costs
estimate_wrong_if: Broken studs requiring drilling/extraction can double the repair cost
Manifold crack vs gasket leak has different part cost

catalytic_converter_failure.dealer_note: OEM converters are expensive. Dealer may not install aftermarket. Confirm before proceeding.
catalytic_converter_failure.diy_time: 1–2 hours (exhaust work requires lift or ramps)

parts.name: Catalytic Converter
parts.oem_note: CARB states: aftermarket converters may not pass smog in CA and other CARB states. Verify local regulations.

uncertainty: Price varies dramatically by make/model/engine
California CARB compliant converters cost significantly more
V6/V8 engines may have two converters
estimate_wrong_if: Do not replace converter until exhaust leaks and O2 sensors are ruled out — very common to replace a good converter
P0420 is the most misdiagnosed code in the industry

parts.name: Downstream O2 Sensor
parts.oem_note: OEM or Denso/Bosch recommended over cheap aftermarket

uncertainty: Sensor may be seized — penetrating oil needed
Wrong sensor fitment wastes money — verify by position and connector type
estimate_wrong_if: Replacing a good sensor because the converter is actually bad will not fix P0420

parts.name: Engine Oil (5 qt)
parts.oem_note: Match exact viscosity in owner's manual — 0W-20 vs 5W-30 matters on VVT engines

estimate_wrong_if: If oil type is correct and code persists, move to VVT solenoid

weak_battery.dealer_note: Some vehicles require battery registration with scan tool after replacement — dealer or specialist needed

parts.name: 12V Car Battery (group size varies)
parts.oem_note: Match group size, CCA rating, and type (AGM for some vehicles)

uncertainty: AGM batteries cost significantly more than standard flooded
Some vehicles require ECU/radio relearn after battery swap
estimate_wrong_if: If alternator is also failing, new battery will discharge again within days
Battery registration required on BMW, Mercedes, some VW/Audi — adds dealer cost

uncertainty: Wheel weights may need replacement if previous ones are missing
estimate_wrong_if: If vibration persists after balance, check for bent rim or worn tire

wheel_bearing.diy_time: 2–4 hours (requires press or hub puller on many applications)

parts.name: Wheel Bearing / Hub Assembly
parts.oem_note: Hub assembly (bolt-on) vs pressed bearing — varies by vehicle

uncertainty: Hub assembly vs pressed-in bearing significantly affects DIY difficulty
Seized axle nuts can add time
estimate_wrong_if: Do not delay wheel bearing replacement — failure can cause wheel to seize or separate
